headlineSummary: HN sentiment skewed negative for Anthropic on mostly critical comments about safety, prompting, and usefulness, with one positive counterexample [E1][E3][E8], while OpenAI also drew a smaller negative read with limited evidence [E6][E7].

ranking.summary: Anthropic drew mostly negative sentiment, with commenters criticizing hardcoded behavior, malware over-avoidance, and unhelpful answers, though one commenter praised Claude Code for a useful workflow boost [E1][E2][E3][E4][E5][E7][E8].
OpenAI was viewed negatively on limited evidence, mainly over financial concerns and one comment calling GPT-5.4 unhelpful, though one comparison was mixed rather than wholly negative [E4][E6][E7].

evidence.excerpt: Claude Code defaulting to a certain set of recommended providers[0] and frameworks is making the web more homogenous and that lack of diversity is increasing the blast radius of incidents

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly names Claude Code and blames its defaults for homogenizing tooling and worsening incident impact.

evidence.excerpt: This should not be Anthropic's hardcoded choice to make. It should be an option, building the system prompt modularily.

annotations.rationale: The commenter directly criticizes Anthropic for enforcing this behavior via a hardcoded system prompt choice.

evidence.excerpt: I'm curious as to why 4.7 seems obsessed with avoiding any actions that could help the user create or enhance malware. The system prompts seem similar on the matter, so I wonder if this is an early attempt by Anthropic to use steering vector injection? The malware paranoia is so strong that my company has had to temporarily block use of 4.7 on our IDE of choice, as the model was behaving in a concerningly unaligned way, as well as spending large amounts of token budget contemplating whether any particular code or task was related to malware development

annotations.rationale: The commenter says Anthropic's 4.7 is overly malware-focused, unusable enough to be blocked internally, and behaving in a concerning way.

evidence.excerpt: Before Opus 4.7, the 4.6 became very much unusable as it has been flagging normal data analysis scripts it wrote itself as cyber security risk. Got several sessions blocked and was unable to finish research with it and had to switch to GPT-5.4 which has its own problems, but at least is not eager to interfere in legitimate work.

annotations.rationale: In the Claude Opus story context, 4.6 is described as unusable and blocking legitimate work.
GPT-5.4 is presented as better than Claude on interference, but the commenter also says it 'has its own problems,' making the assessment mixed.

evidence.excerpt: I'm fascinated that Anthropic employees, who are supposed to be the LLM experts, are using tricks like these which go against how LLMs seem to work.

annotations.rationale: The commenter questions Anthropic employees' competence or judgment in designing prompts that allegedly conflict with how LLMs work.

evidence.excerpt: OpenAI is currently mired in a capital crunch. Their last round just about sucked all the dry powder out of the private markets. Folks are now starting to ask difficult questions about their burn rate and revenue. It is increasingly looking like they might not commit to the purchase order they made which kick-started this whole panic over RAM.

annotations.rationale: The comment portrays OpenAI as financially strained and potentially unable to honor a major purchase commitment.

evidence.excerpt: No luck with LLMs since it's such a common question today the answers I get from GPT-5.4 and Claude 4.6 Opus with search are unhelpful.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly says GPT-5.4's answers were unhelpful.
The commenter explicitly says Claude 4.6 Opus with search gave unhelpful answers.

evidence.excerpt: I've had excellent luck using Claude Code to generate "mermaid diagrams" for me, and convert them to .png format headlessly using mmdc/puppeteer. Really helped me out with an engineering proposal I just finished. In past years I would have fumbled around with Visio forever and the result would have been worse.

annotations.rationale: The commenter strongly praises Claude Code's usefulness and says it materially improved their workflow and output.
